meta.title: Cardio-Respiratory Arrest Adult (signs of puberty are visible)
notes: Timing swim-lane: rhythm analysis '1 min. or less', each CPR cycle block '2 min.', then 'anticipation of evacuation' / 'continuous assessment'.
The quality of the compression is a priority (Class I, LoE B).
After 2 min. compressions-ventilations, replace the person doing chest compressions (ERC, 2015).
Limit ventilation volume; observe normal lifting of the chest (risk of gastric filling and silent vomiting).
Follow the SARC instructions in case of a deceased person.
When possible, allow the family to see that the first responders are doing whatever is possible to save the patient's life.
[1] = 'check for signs of life' (the recurring footnote referenced after resuming CPR).

cpr_01.text: Patient evaluation: no sign of life. (Unresponsive; open the airway chin lift / jaw thrust; check-listen-feel max 10 sec; gasping is not considered efficient breathing; don't waste time checking pulse; evidence of trauma: consider maintaining the head-neck-trunk axis until final immobilisation.)

cpr_02.text: CPR 30:2 until AED is ready. (30 compressions: push hard and fast in the middle of the chest 5-6 cm but not more than 6 cm, 2 hands, rate 100-120/min, full chest recoil, minimise interruptions; 2 ventilations with BVM + oxygen in max 10 sec; if possible use an oropharyngeal cannula; manual suction device ready.)

cpr_03.text: Rhythm analysis (round 1). Do not touch the patient during analysis.

cpr_04.text: 1 shock, then CPR 30:2 for 2 min / 5-6 cycles. (Do not touch the patient during analysis and shock. After resuming CPR, check for signs of life [1].)

cpr_05.text: Shock not indicated: CPR 30:2 for 2 min / 5-6 cycles. (After resuming CPR, check for signs of life [1].)

cpr_06.text: Rhythm analysis (round 2). Switch roles for compressions / ventilations. Do not touch the patient during analysis.

cpr_07.text: 1 shock, then CPR 30:2 for 2 min / 5-6 cycles. (Do not touch the patient during analysis and shock. After resuming CPR, check for signs of life [1].)

cpr_08.text: Shock not indicated: CPR 30:2 for 2 min / 5-6 cycles. (After resuming CPR, check for signs of life [1].)

cpr_09.text: Rhythm analysis (round 3). Switch roles for compressions / ventilations. Do not touch the patient during shock.

cpr_10.text: 1 shock, then CPR 30:2. Prepare for evacuation. (After resuming CPR, check for signs of life [1].)

cpr_13.text: Evacuation from the place of intervention to the ambulance: find the best balance between minimal interruption of chest compressions and moving to the ambulance in order to quickly reach the appropriate care center for advanced care.

cpr_15.text: Maintain SpO2 between 94-98%; in any case, nonrebreather mask 10-15 l/min (according to the SARC Manual Training & Oxygen Administration Protocol); continue to monitor closely (possible new cardiac arrest); keep temperature between 32-36 C.

cpr_19.text: Transport to an appropriate health center (cardiology).
